title: MAP systems-adherence audit + emergence ID-allocation race fix
role: MAP process auditor and corrective implementer
description: Operator asked to review whether MAP processes are being used properly and find room for improvement (hcom #24367). Split with codex-lab-neko (TASK-140 covers operating-loop/mechanics); this task covers systems-adherence: which of the ~15 documented systems are genuinely exercised vs specification-only, building on TASK-129/130's prior audit. Found and fixed a real gap the prior audit missed: map_emergence.py's create-artifact ID allocation (next_id()) had no lock at all, unlike what TASK-129's Finding 2 assumed -- reproduced a genuine ID collision under concurrent creation, then fixed it with a per-kind file lock (MAP_System/.locks/emergence-<prefix>.lock), verified the fix closes the race, and confirmed the two previously-recommended-but-never-built follow-ups (map_repair.py ID allocation, a second Retrospective record) are still open. Also files a standalone RETRO-0002 for today's ProjectUpdater/CommandCenterUI cycle (TASK-135-139), since the last audit found Retrospective specifically under-used.
acceptance_criteria: map_emergence.py's create-artifact ID allocation is race-safe under concurrent invocation (demonstrated by a before/after reproduction, not just re-running the existing test suite)
Audit artifact distinguishes genuinely-exercised systems from documented-but-unused ones, cross-referencing TASK-129/130 rather than re-deriving from scratch
A standalone RETRO-0002 record exists for the TASK-135-139 cycle using templates/RETROSPECTIVE_TEMPLATE.md
Existing map_emergence.py tests and the full MAP validator suite still pass
Task is submitted for independent review, not self-approved
